---
title: ""
format:
  typst:
    margin:
      x: "0.85in"
      y: "0.5in"
    fontsize: 10pt
    mainfont: "Libertinus Serif"
    sansfont: "Libertinus Sans"
bibliography: []
citeproc: false
---

```{=typst}
#set page(numbering: "1")
#set par(justify: true, leading: 0.5em)
#set text(size: 10pt)
#set list(spacing: 0.35em)
#set block(spacing: 0.62em)

// Section heading style
#show heading.where(level: 1): it => {
  set text(size: 11pt, weight: "bold")
  v(0.2em)
  upper(text(tracking: 0.1em, it.body))
  v(-0.25em)
  line(length: 100%, stroke: 0.5pt)
  v(0.12em)
}

#show heading.where(level: 2): it => {
  set text(size: 10pt, weight: "bold")
  v(0.12em)
  it.body
  v(0.03em)
}

#align(center)[
  #text(size: 20pt, weight: "bold")[Emily K. Johnson]
  \
  #text(size: 10pt)[Health economist specializing in causal inference and register-based analysis of the long-term economic and health impacts of disease.]
  \
  #link("https://www.linkedin.com/in/emilykatherinejohnson/")[LinkedIn]
  | #link("https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Emily-Johnson-107")[ResearchGate]
]
```

# Education

```{=typst}
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [*University of Southern Denmark*], [Odense, DK,]
)
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [_Doctor of Philosophy in Health Economics_], [Expected 2026]
)
```

- Affiliations: Danish Center for Health Economics, Danish Institute for Advanced Study
- Supervised by Angela Chang, DSc, Liza Sopina, PhD, and Harsh Parikh, PhD

```{=typst}
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [*Johns Hopkins University*], [Baltimore, MD, USA,]
)
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [_Visiting Graduate Student_], [January 2026 – April 2026]
)
```

```{=typst}
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [*London School of Economics*], [London, UK,  Level: Distinction]
)
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [_Master of Science in Health Data Science_], [July 2023]
)
```

- *Prize for Excellence in MSc Health Data Science* – Highest Average 2022/23

```{=typst}
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [*University of Edinburgh*], [Edinburgh, UK,]
)
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [_Postgraduate Certificate in Epidemiology_], [September 2023]
)
```

```{=typst}
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [*Rice University*], [Houston, TX, USA,  GPA: 3.52]
)
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [_Bachelor of Arts in Statistics, Minor in Global Health Technologies_], [May 2017]
)
```

# Research Experience

```{=typst}
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [*University of Southern Denmark, Danish Center for Health Economics*], [Odense, DK]
)
```
```{=typst}
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [_Graduate student – Experienced Burden of Disease Project_], [2023 – Present]
)
```

- Estimating the long-term economic, social, and health losses attributable to breast cancer, stroke, alcohol use disorder, and depression over an 18-year period in Denmark, using register-based causal inference (staggered difference-in-differences and event-study methods) on linked administrative health, education, and economic data.
- Working paper: *Illness and Family Formation: Evidence from Danish Register Data* (in preparation).

```{=typst}
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [_Graduate student – Predicting Early Mortality Project_], [2023 – Present]
)
```

- Identifying and characterizing individuals who die prematurely in Denmark using machine learning and decomposition methods across over 2,000 individual-level health, economic, demographic, and social characteristics.
- Working paper: *Predicting and Characterizing Premature Mortality in Denmark* (in preparation).

```{=typst}
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [_Contributor – International Network on Comparative Resource Use_], [2024 – Present]
)
```

- Comparing hospital resource use by disease, age, sex, and comorbidity in Denmark against 16 other high-income countries, with the University of Oxford and the Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation.

```{=typst}
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [*University of Washington, Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation*], [Seattle, WA]
)
```
```{=typst}
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [_Research Scientist I – US Disease Expenditure Project_], [2021 – 2023]
)
```

- Estimated US county- and state-level disease-specific health expenditure by payer and type of care, 2000–2019, using billions of rows of administrative data with econometric and small-area estimation methods.

```{=typst}
#grid(columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right),
  [_Research Scientist I – Global Burden of Disease, Clinical Informatics_], [2019 – 2021]
)
```

- Estimated incidence and prevalence for 85 diseases and injuries through Bayesian mixed-effects modeling of hospital administrative data from over 30 countries, a key input to GBD morbidity estimation.

# Teaching

- *Lecturer, examiner, and course administrator* – Global Health Policy graduate course, University of Southern Denmark (Spring 2024).
- *MSc thesis supervision* – MSc Data Science, 4 students, University of Southern Denmark (Spring 2024, 2025).

# Skills

- 8 years in R (data.table, arrow, dplyr, ggplot), 6 in Python (pandas, arrow), and 3 in SQL; experienced with parquet, Spark, and distributed computation on data at the scale of billions of rows and tens of terabytes.
- Familiar with Claude Code, Elicit, and agentic AI workflows. Conversant in Danish.
